questionHtml:
Where does oxidation occur in a voltaic cell?

A. positive electrode and anode

B. negative electrode and anode

C. positive electrode and cathode

D. negative electrode and cathode

meta.title: Where does oxidation occur in a voltaic cell? A. positive electrode and anode B. negative electrode and anode C. positive electrode and cathode D. negative electrode and cathode
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

Reactivity 3.2.5—Oxidation occurs at the anode and reduction occurs at the cathode in electrochemical cells. Identify electrodes as anode and cathode, and identify their signs/polarities in voltaic cells and electrolytic cells, based on the type of reaction occurring at the electrode.
